name: Encora Inc.
description: Lead front-end development on a large-scale enterprise platform for a US-based client, working within a 10+ person cross-functional team serving hundreds of users. Inherited a codebase with no clear separation between front-end and back-end concerns — architected and implemented a near-complete layer separation, dramatically improving maintainability, testability, and team velocity.

projects.name: Enterprise Platform
projects.description: A large-scale enterprise platform serving hundreds of users across a US-based client organization.
mainTasks: Lead front-end development within a 10+ person cross-functional team.
Architected and implemented a near-complete front-end/back-end layer separation.
Dramatically improved maintainability, testability, and team velocity.
Integrated AI tools into daily workflow for task breakdown, sprint planning, and architecture decisions.

name: CI&T
description: Contributed to a B2B product with a front-end team of 10+ developers sharing a single monorepo — navigated large-scale codebase coordination, enforced UI consistency across modules, and contributed to a shared design system.

projects.name: B2B Monorepo Platform
projects.description: A B2B product built on a single monorepo shared by a large front-end team.
mainTasks: Navigated large-scale codebase coordination across 10+ developers.
Enforced UI consistency across multiple modules.
Contributed to a shared design system.

name: Dextra (acquired by CI&T)
description: Originally Dextra, acquired by CI&T. Maintained an online marketplace seller software built in Angular 2+ (TypeScript) with its own design system, working fully remote.

projects.name: Online Marketplace
projects.description: Responsible for maintaining and expanding the seller's section — full of forms, tables, and other methods of presenting data.
mainTasks: Maintaining an updated environment by keeping the code up to date with its main branch.
Updating older areas with new and default components and updated design.
Creating new areas and components following a Design System.
Always searching for new ways to improve.
Revising and sending my own Pull Requests.

name: HarboR Informática Industrial
description: Led a 3-person front-end team building shop floor engineering software for the manufacturing industry, delivering stable, long-running enterprise interfaces in AngularJS. Managed development lifecycle from planning to delivery across multiple concurrent projects.

projects.name: LiveMes
projects.description: An online platform where the client could see in real time all the information collected in his shop floor. Through sensors, the client would collect and send us all the information gathered from his machines, and by using creative components the client could see, analyze and take actions on the information gained.
mainTasks: Create the project from the ground up.
Define the system's architecture.
Design and implement the look and feel of the web app.
Create new and intuitive ways to show important information.
Keep the project up to date, light and running in all possible devices.
Make all the components 100% responsive.
Keep in touch with part of the team located across the country.
projects.extra: With this project I brought the company the importance of a well-kept front-end, and to appreciate a good looking software.

projects.name: PrintServer
projects.description: Internal-use software for a washing machine manufacturer, tracking each product through its testing phase and printing results for traceability.
mainTasks: Create from the ground up the project's front-end.
Define the system's architecture.
Analyze the client's website and products to create a matching design.
Make it compatible with very old Internet Explorer versions.
projects.extra: Created an extremely lightweight and reliable web app capable of running in the client's old shop floor computers.

name: Primesoft
description: Developed custom websites for clients from scratch and via WordPress. Maintained an online test-taking web app using JavaScript, jQuery and PHP. Also created digital banners and flyers using Adobe Photoshop and Illustrator.

projects.name: Prova da Ordem
projects.description: An online test taking app where Brazilian lawyers could study and practice for their OAB exam. At its time it was innovative and futuristic.
mainTasks: Maintaining the already existing project.
Creating new areas and components.
Creating banners, flyers and other visual elements for the brand.
Keeping the website's FTP always updated.
Developing back-end functions when needed (server side rendered).
projects.extra: Through my creativity, my bosses saw my potential and approved me to recreate the whole project's visual, bringing it a new and fresh look. Here was where my JavaScript abilities flourished.

projects.name: Custom Websites
projects.description: Created custom websites for various clients. Some would come with a design, others I would build over website templates.
mainTasks: Create basic responsive websites.
Create custom pictures, illustrations and icons.
Maintain updated through FTP servers.
projects.extra: With my creativity the clients always left happy and fulfilled.

name: Outplan
description: Client support and SQL script developer. While understanding the inner workings of the software to test it, I also created complex SQL queries to generate charts and data sheets.

name: CIDASC
description: Outsourced tech support via Bonatec. From fixing printers to solving tech crises by phone with farmers all across the state of Santa Catarina.

name: CDI (Now CPDI)
description: Voluntary work receiving donations of old computers from big companies and getting them running again to minimum Windows XP standards. These computers were placed in CDI stations across Brazil, giving underprivileged kids and adults access to technology and the internet.
